entries.name: agent-builder
entries.description:
Design and build AI agents for any domain. Use when users:
(1) ask to "create an agent", "build an assistant", or "design an AI system"
(2) want to understand agent architecture, agentic patterns, or autonomous AI
(3) need help with capabilities, subagents, planning, or skill mechanisms
(4) ask about Claude Code, Cursor, or similar agent internals
(5) want to build agents for business, research, creative, or operational tasks
Keywords: agent, assistant, autonomous, workflow, tool use, multi-step, orchestration

entries.name: api-design
entries.description: REST API design patterns including resource naming, status codes, pagination, filtering, error responses, versioning, and rate limiting for production APIs.

entries.name: architecture-decision-records
entries.description: Capture architectural decisions made during Claude Code sessions as structured ADRs. Auto-detects decision moments, records context, alternatives considered, and rationale. Maintains an ADR log so future developers understand why the codebase is shaped the way it is.

entries.name: backend-patterns
entries.description: Backend architecture patterns, API design, database optimization, and server-side best practices for Node.js, Express, and Next.js API routes.

entries.name: bambu-labs
entries.description: Dry-run, upload, and cautiously initiate local Bambu Lab print jobs from validated plain `.gcode`, using Bambu LAN FTPS/MQTT handoffs.

entries.name: brainstorming
entries.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

entries.name: cad
entries.description: Create, modify, inspect, and validate STEP-first parametric CAD parts and assemblies. Use for natural-language CAD specs, reference images, 2D technical drawings, STEP/STP generation or direct inspection, Python CAD source, source-level joints, selector references, geometry facts, measurements, mating deltas, snapshots, and secondary STL/3MF/native GLB outputs from CAD geometry.

entries.name: cad-viewer
entries.description: Start or reuse CAD Viewer and return review links for explicit CAD, implicit CAD, robot-description, and G-code files. Use when visually reviewing `.step`, `.stp`, `.implicit.js`, `.implicit.mjs`, `.glb`, `.stl`, `.3mf`, `.gcode`, `.dxf`, `.urdf`, `.srdf`, or `.sdf` files, especially when handed off from CAD, implicit-cad, G-code, URDF, SRDF, or SDF generation skills.

entries.name: canary
entries.description:
Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,
performance regressions, and page failures using the browse daemon. Takes
periodic screenshots, compares against pre-deploy baselines, and alerts
on anomalies. Use when: "monitor deploy", "canary", "post-deploy check",
"watch production", "verify deploy".

entries.name: code-review
entries.description: Perform thorough code reviews with security, performance, and maintainability analysis. Use when user asks to review code, check for bugs, or audit a codebase.

entries.name: codebase-onboarding
entries.description: Analyze an unfamiliar codebase and generate a structured onboarding guide with architecture map, key entry points, conventions, and a starter CLAUDE.md. Use when joining a new project or setting up Claude Code for the first time in a repo.

entries.name: database-migrations
entries.description: Database migration best practices for schema changes, data migrations, rollbacks, and zero-downtime deployments across PostgreSQL, MySQL, and common ORMs (Prisma, Drizzle, Kysely, Django, TypeORM, golang-migrate).

entries.name: deep-research
entries.description: Multi-source deep research using firecrawl and exa MCPs. Searches the web, synthesizes findings, and delivers cited reports with source attribution. Use when the user wants thorough research on any topic with evidence and citations.

entries.name: defuddle
entries.description: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using Defuddle CLI, removing clutter and navigation to save tokens. Use instead of WebFetch when the user provides a URL to read or analyze, for online documentation, articles, blog posts, or any standard web page. Do NOT use for URLs ending in .md — those are already markdown, use WebFetch directly.

entries.name: deployment-patterns
entries.description: Deployment workflows, CI/CD pipeline patterns, Docker containerization, health checks, rollback strategies, and production readiness checklists for web applications.

entries.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
entries.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

entries.name: docker-patterns
entries.description: Docker and Docker Compose patterns for local development, container security, networking, volume strategies, and multi-service orchestration.

entries.name: dxf
entries.description: Generate, regenerate, and validate 2D DXF drawings from Python ezdxf sources. Use for DXF files, gen_dxf() sources, 2D profiles, outlines, templates, gaskets, panels, flat patterns, laser/plasma/waterjet cut layouts, and 2D drawing exports of CAD geometry.

entries.name: e2e-testing
entries.description: Playwright E2E testing patterns, Page Object Model, configuration, CI/CD integration, artifact management, and flaky test strategies.

entries.name: executing-plans
entries.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

entries.name: finishing-a-development-branch
entries.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

entries.name: frontend-patterns
entries.description: Frontend development patterns for React, Next.js, state management, performance optimization, and UI best practices.

entries.name: gcode
entries.description: Generate, inspect, dry-run, and statically validate plain FDM `.gcode` from 3D mesh files by orchestrating real slicer CLIs. Use when Codex needs to slice `.stl`, `.obj`, unsliced `.3mf`, `.ply`, `.glb`, or `.gltf` into printer-profiled G-code, discover local slicer backends, inspect whether a mesh is slice-ready, or validate generated G-code before any printer-specific handoff.

entries.name: golang-patterns
entries.description: Idiomatic Go patterns, best practices, and conventions for building robust, efficient, and maintainable Go applications.

entries.name: golang-testing
entries.description: Go testing patterns including table-driven tests, subtests, benchmarks, fuzzing, and test coverage. Follows TDD methodology with idiomatic Go practices.

entries.name: guard
entries.description:
Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits.
Combines /careful (warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, etc.) with
/freeze (blocks edits outside a specified directory). Use for maximum safety
when touching prod or debugging live systems. Use when asked to "guard mode",
"full safety", "lock it down", or "maximum safety".

entries.name: implicit-cad
entries.description: Create, edit, render, and snapshot browser-native implicit CAD `.implicit.js` and `.implicit.mjs` files using GLSL signed-distance fields, shader primitives, smooth booleans, TPMS fields, and direct CAD Viewer raymarch rendering. Experimental.

entries.name: investigate
entries.description:
Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. Four phases: investigate,
analyze, hypothesize, implement. Iron Law: no fixes without root cause.
Use when asked to "debug this", "fix this bug", "why is this broken",
"investigate this error", or "root cause analysis".
Proactively suggest when the user reports errors, unexpected behavior, or
is troubleshooting why something stopped working.

entries.name: json-canvas
entries.description: Create and edit JSON Canvas files (.canvas) with nodes, edges, groups, and connections. Use when working with .canvas files, creating visual canvases, mind maps, flowcharts, or when the user mentions Canvas files in Obsidian.

entries.name: land-and-deploy
entries.description:
Land and deploy workflow. Merges the PR, waits for CI and deploy,
verifies production health via canary checks. Takes over after /ship
creates the PR. Use when: "merge", "land", "deploy", "merge and verify",
"land it", "ship it to production".

entries.name: mcp-server-patterns
entries.description: Build MCP servers with Node/TypeScript SDK — tools, resources, prompts, Zod validation, stdio vs Streamable HTTP. Use Context7 or official MCP docs for latest API.

entries.name: obsidian-bases
entries.description: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.

entries.name: obsidian-cli
entries.description: Interact with Obsidian vaults using the Obsidian CLI to read, create, search, and manage notes, tasks, properties, and more. Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DOM. Use when the user asks to interact with their Obsidian vault, manage notes, search vault content, perform vault operations from the command line, or develop and debug Obsidian plugins and themes.

entries.name: obsidian-markdown
entries.description: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax. Use when working with .md files in Obsidian, or when the user mentions wikilinks, callouts, frontmatter, tags, embeds, or Obsidian notes.

entries.name: opencv-patterns
entries.description: OpenCV best practices for computer vision pipelines — image I/O, color spaces, geometric transforms, video decoding, contour analysis, and GPU-accelerated operations with CUDA. Use when writing or reviewing OpenCV-based image/video processing code.

entries.name: pdf
entries.description: Process PDF files - extract text, create PDFs, merge documents. Use when user asks to read PDF, create PDF, or work with PDF files.

entries.name: postgres-patterns
entries.description: PostgreSQL database patterns for query optimization, schema design, indexing, and security. Based on Supabase best practices.

entries.name: python-patterns
entries.description: Pythonic idioms, PEP 8 standards, type hints, and best practices for building robust, efficient, and maintainable Python applications.

entries.name: python-testing
entries.description: Python testing strategies using pytest, TDD methodology, fixtures, mocking, parametrization, and coverage requirements.

entries.name: pytorch-patterns
entries.description: PyTorch deep learning patterns and best practices for building robust, efficient, and reproducible training pipelines, model architectures, and data loading.

entries.name: qa
entries.description:
Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. Runs QA testing,
then iteratively fixes bugs in source code, committing each fix atomically and
re-verifying. Use when asked to "qa", "QA", "test this site", "find bugs",
"test and fix", or "fix what's broken".
Proactively suggest when the user says a feature is ready for testing
or asks "does this work?". Three tiers: Quick (critical/high only),
Standard (+ medium), Exhaustive (+ cosmetic). Produces before/after health scores,
fix evidence, and a ship-readiness summary. For report-only mode, use /qa-only.

entries.name: receiving-code-review
entries.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

entries.name: requesting-code-review
entries.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

entries.name: ros1-expert
entries.description:
Use when working on ROS1 (Noetic) robotics projects: nodes, topics, services,
actions, TF/TF2, debugging tools, rosbag workflows, sensor calibration
(LiDAR + camera + IMU), common error fixes, and launch file patterns.
Use when building ROS1 packages with colcon build, diagnosing TF errors,
replaying rosbags, writing CMakeLists.txt with install() directives, or
working on calibration pipelines (LiDAR-camera, camera-IMU, LiDAR-IMU).
Keywords: ros1, noetic, roslaunch, rostopic, rosbag, tf, nodelet, catkin,
colcon, sensor fusion, lidar camera calibration, imu calibration.

entries.name: ros2-engineering-skills
entries.description: TRIGGER when the user: writes or reviews ROS 2 nodes (rclcpp/rclpy), creates packages (colcon/ament), edits launch files (.launch.py), configures QoS or DDS, writes URDF/xacro, implements ros2_control hardware interfaces or controllers, sets up Nav2/MoveIt 2 pipelines, processes sensor data (camera/LiDAR/PCL), works with Gazebo/Isaac Sim, configures SROS2 security, develops micro-ROS firmware, manages multi-robot fleets (Open-RMF), debugs with ros2 doctor/rosbag2, deploys via Docker/cross-compilation, or migrates from ROS 1. DO NOT TRIGGER for general C++/Python questions unrelated to ROS 2, non-robotics middleware, or web/mobile development tasks.

entries.name: ros2-engineering-skills
entries.description: TRIGGER when the user: writes or reviews ROS 2 nodes (rclcpp/rclpy), creates packages (colcon/ament), edits launch files (.launch.py), configures QoS or DDS, writes URDF/xacro, implements ros2_control hardware interfaces or controllers, sets up Nav2/MoveIt 2 pipelines, processes sensor data (camera/LiDAR/PCL), works with Gazebo/Isaac Sim, configures SROS2 security, develops micro-ROS firmware, manages multi-robot fleets (Open-RMF), debugs with ros2 doctor/rosbag2, deploys via Docker/cross-compilation, or migrates from ROS 1. DO NOT TRIGGER for general C++/Python questions unrelated to ROS 2, non-robotics middleware, or web/mobile development tasks.

entries.name: sdf
entries.description: SDFormat/SDF model and world generation, validation, and simulator handoff. Use for `.sdf` files, SDFormat XML, Python `gen_sdf()` sources, models, worlds, links, joints, poses, frames, inertials, visual/collision geometry, mesh URIs, sensors, lights, physics, plugins, includes, Gazebo, static SDF review, or simulator-specific metadata. Do not use for signed-distance-field geometry.

entries.name: security-review
entries.description: Use this skill when adding authentication, handling user input, working with secrets, creating API endpoints, or implementing payment/sensitive features. Provides comprehensive security checklist and patterns.

entries.name: security-scan
entries.description: Scan your Claude Code configuration (.claude/ directory) for security vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and injection risks using AgentShield. Checks CLAUDE.md, settings.json, MCP servers, hooks, and agent definitions.

entries.name: sendcutsend
entries.description: Review DXF and STEP/STP uploads for SendCutSend.com orders using its ordering guide, catalog, and specs. Use only for SendCutSend.com preflight reports covering upload readiness, selected material/SKU/thickness/service availability, and service-specific checks for laser cutting, CNC routing, bending, tapping, countersinking, hardware insertion, and finishing.

entries.name: ship
entries.description:
Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION, update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR. Use when asked to "ship", "deploy", "push to main", "create a PR", or "merge and push".
Proactively suggest when the user says code is ready or asks about deploying.

entries.name: srdf
entries.description: MoveIt2 SRDF generation, validation, and planning-semantics workflow. Use when creating, editing, regenerating, inspecting, or validating `.srdf` files, `gen_srdf()` sources, MoveIt planning groups, virtual joints, passive joints, end effectors, group states, disabled collisions, URDF-linked planning semantics, or SRDF handoff for live review. Use the URDF skill for robot structure, the SDF skill for simulator descriptions, and the cad-viewer skill for rendering, live review links, and optional MoveIt2 controls.

entries.name: step-parts
entries.description: Find, evaluate, and download common purchasable CAD parts from step.parts, including named off-the-shelf actuators, servos, motors, electronics boards, connectors, screws, bolts, nuts, washers, bearings, standoffs, and other catalog components. Use when Codex needs to search the hosted step.parts catalog before creating simplified placeholder geometry, resolve fuzzy part names, standards, aliases, or dimensions, choose a matching part, fetch a canonical .step file, verify checksums, or use the step.parts API/OpenAPI/catalog endpoints for standard part discovery.

entries.name: subagent-driven-development
entries.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

entries.name: systematic-debugging
entries.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

entries.name: tdd-workflow
entries.description: Use this skill when writing new features, fixing bugs, or refactoring code. Enforces test-driven development with 80%+ coverage including unit, integration, and E2E tests.

entries.name: test-driven-development
entries.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

entries.name: urdf
entries.description: URDF robot description generation and default generation-time validation. Use when creating, editing, regenerating, inspecting, or debugging `.urdf` files, Python `gen_urdf()` sources, robot links, joints, limits, inertials, visual/collision geometry, mesh references, frame conventions, or generated robot-description artifacts. Use the SRDF skill for MoveIt2 semantic groups and IK/path-planning semantics; use the cad-viewer skill for local MoveIt2 server controls; use the CAD skill for STEP/STL/3MF/DXF/GLB outputs.

entries.name: using-git-worktrees
entries.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

entries.name: using-superpowers
entries.description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

entries.name: verification-before-completion
entries.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

entries.name: writing-plans
entries.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

entries.name: writing-skills
entries.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

entries.name: yolo-object-detection
entries.description: YOLOv8/YOLO object detection patterns using Ultralytics — inference, training, SAHI sliced inference for small objects, export (ONNX/TensorRT), and ROS 2 integration. Use when building or debugging YOLO-based detection pipelines.

entries.name: code-reviewer
entries.description: Use this agent when a major project step has been completed and needs to be reviewed against the original plan and coding standards. Examples: <example>Context: The user is creating a code-review agent that should be called after a logical chunk of code is written. user: "I've finished implementing the user authentication system as outlined in step 3 of our plan" assistant: "Great work! Now let me use the code-reviewer agent to review the implementation against our plan and coding standards" <commentary>Since a major project step has been completed, use the code-reviewer agent to validate the work against the plan and identify any issues.</commentary></example> <example>Context: User has completed a significant feature implementation. user: "The API endpoints for the task management system are now complete - that covers step 2 from our architecture document" assistant: "Excellent! Let me have the code-reviewer agent examine this implementation to ensure it aligns with our plan and follows best practices" <commentary>A numbered step from the planning document has been completed, so the code-reviewer agent should review the work.</commentary></example>

entries.name: gsd-advisor-researcher
entries.description: Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode.
entries.tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*

entries.name: gsd-codebase-mapper
entries.description: Explores codebase and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned by map-codebase with a focus area (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load.

entries.name: gsd-debugger
entries.description: Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd:debug orchestrator.
entries.tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch

entries.name: gsd-executor
entries.description: Executes GSD plans with atomic commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and state management. Spawned by execute-phase orchestrator or execute-plan command.
entries.tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob

entries.name: gsd-plan-checker
entries.description: Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.

entries.name: gsd-planner
entries.description: Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.
entries.tools: Read, Write, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*

entries.name: gsd-verifier
entries.description: Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.

entries.name: Librarian
entries.description: AI Librarian for Agent Hub — discover, recommend, and publish skills to your project. Use when you want to find the right skills, understand what's available, or install skills from the curated library.

collections.name: Debugging Deep Dive
collections.description: Bo ky nang debug chuyen sau, tu phan tich root cause den verify fix va review. Dung khi gap bug kho, production issue, hoac loi khong ro nguyen nhan.
collections.curator_notes: Khong bao gio patch symptom ma khong tim root cause. systematic-debugging la buoc bat buoc dau tien. investigate dung cho cac bug phuc tap can dao sau vao code. gsd-debugger huong dan tu dong hypotheses neu ban bi stuck.

entries.role: Framework debug co he thong: thu thap evidence, form hypotheses, test methodically
entries.when: Luon bat dau o day truoc khi thu bat ky fix nao — day la Iron Law

entries.role: Deep dive vao codebase de hieu flow va tim nguon goc van de
entries.when: Khi can hieu code khong quen hoac bug co nhieu potential causes

entries.role: Agent tu dong sinh hypotheses va test chung theo thu tu
entries.when: Khi ban da stuck hon 30 phut hoac bug phuc tap voi nhieu interacting components

entries.role: Vet ban do dependency va data flow cua codebase de hieu context day du
entries.when: Khi bug lien quan den nhieu module hoac kho trace call chain

entries.role: Dam bao fix khong gay regression va tat ca related tests van pass
entries.when: Sau khi ap dung fix, truoc khi close issue

entries.role: Review fix cung dong nghiep — bug fix thuong co edge cases an
entries.when: Khi fix anh huong den logic quan trong hoac core path

collections.name: DevOps Ship Cycle
collections.description: Quy trinh ship code an toan tu review, security scan, QA den deploy va monitor. Dam bao moi release an toan, co the rollback, va duoc theo doi sau deploy.
collections.curator_notes: Khong bao gio ship ma khong qua security-scan va qa. canary deploy truoc khi full rollout. guard la buoc bat buoc de detect van de sow deploy. Sequence nay giam incident rate dang ke.

entries.role: Peer review code truoc bat ky buoc ship nao — gate dau tien
entries.when: Khi code ready, truoc tat ca cac buoc khac trong ship cycle

entries.role: Quet OWASP vulnerabilities, dependency CVE, secrets leak truoc khi merge
entries.when: Truoc moi merge vao main/production branch

entries.role: Quality assurance toan dien: functional, regression, performance, edge cases
entries.when: Sau code review va security scan deu pass

entries.role: Dong goi app theo docker best practices de dam bao consistency giua envs
entries.when: Khi build artifact cho deployment

entries.role: Deploy an toan voi blue-green, feature flags, health checks, rollback plan
entries.when: Khi chuan bi deploy len staging hoac production

entries.role: Checklist ship cuoi cung: announce, monitor dashboards, be ready for rollback
entries.when: Ngay truoc va sau khi deploy

entries.role: Roll out dan dan voi traffic routing, monitor metrics truoc full release
entries.when: Cho production deployments anh huong nhieu users

entries.role: Monitor sau deploy: alerts, error rates, latency, business metrics
entries.when: Sau moi deployment len production, it nhat 24-48 gio

collections.name: Plan-Driven Development
collections.description: Quy trinh phat trien day du voi planning nghiem tuc, architecture decisions, va execution co to chuc. Phu hop cho feature lon, refactor lon, hoac he thong phuc tap.
collections.curator_notes: Plan truoc, code sau. Collection nay tranh technical debt va re-work do thieu planning. architecture-decision-records quan trong cho decisons lon. gsd-planner giup chia nho task de execute. subagent-driven-development dung cho task co the chay song song.

entries.role: Kham pha problem space, constraints, trade-offs truoc khi commit vao approach nao
entries.when: Bat dau moi feature phuc tap hoac refactor quan trong

entries.role: Document cac architectural decisions quan trong voi context, options, va rationale
entries.when: Khi co decision anh huong den cau truc he thong hoac khong de reverse

entries.role: Lap ke hoach chi tiet voi tasks, acceptance criteria, va dependencies
entries.when: Sau khi architecture da ro rang, truoc khi bat dau code

entries.role: Agent tu dong chia plan thanh sub-tasks co the execute, estimate complexity
entries.when: Khi plan phuc tap voi nhieu dependencies hoac team member

entries.role: Thuc thi tung task theo plan, khong skip steps, khong improvise ngoai plan
entries.when: Khi bat dau implement

entries.role: Chay nhieu tasks doc lap song song qua subagents de tang toc do
entries.when: Khi co nhieu task doc lap co the chay cung luc

entries.role: Review voi context day du ve plan goc va architectural decisions
entries.when: Khi implementation xong, truoc khi merge

entries.role: Xac nhan tat ca acceptance criteria da duoc dap ung, khong co regression
entries.when: Truoc khi close task hoac merge PR

collections.name: TDD-First Development
collections.description: Bo ky nang phat trien theo quy trinh TDD day du, tu giai doan hieu yeu cau den commit cuoi cung. Ap dung khi bat dau feature moi hoac refactor lon.
collections.curator_notes: Day la collection nen ap dung cho moi feature moi. Bat dau voi brainstorming de tranh bi go sai huong ngay tu dau, sau do viet test truoc khi code. Sequence nay da duoc chung minh giam bug rate 40-60% trong thuc te.

entries.role: Kham pha yeu cau va tim hieu van de truoc khi viet bat ky dong code nao
entries.when: Luon bat dau o buoc nay — ngay ca task nho cung can 5-10 phut brainstorm

entries.role: Lap ke hoach cu the: test cases can viet, acceptance criteria, step-by-step implementation
entries.when: Sau khi brainstorm xong va hieu ro yeu cau

entries.role: Quy trinh TDD core: viet test -> fail -> implement -> pass -> refactor
entries.when: Khi bat dau implement bat ky logic business nao

entries.role: Pattern chi tiet hon cho TDD: mock strategy, fixture design, test organization
entries.when: Khi can huong dan cu the hon ve cach to chuc test suite

entries.role: Thuc thi ke hoach theo tung task, check-off khi xong, khong nhay coc
entries.when: Khi bat dau implement theo plan da co

entries.role: Chuan bi code review request ro rang voi context va cau hoi cu the
entries.when: Khi tat ca tests pass va code ready for review

entries.role: Kiem tra lan cuoi: tests, lint, types, no regressions truoc khi close
entries.when: Truoc khi mark task la done hoac merge PR

entries.role: Cleanup branch: squash commits, update changelog, remove debug code
entries.when: Truoc khi merge vao main branch

stacks.name: API Service Go
stacks.description: Bo kit cho backend API service viet bang Go. Bao gom Go patterns, testing, API design, database, security va full ship cycle.

core_skills.why: Go idioms — error handling, interfaces, goroutines, context, struct patterns.
Go testing — table-driven tests, testify, mock interfaces, benchmarks.
REST API design — routing, middleware, error responses, versioning cho Go services.
PostgreSQL với Go — sqlx/pgx, connection pooling, migrations, query optimization.
API security — input validation, SQL injection, auth/authz, dependency audits.
Multi-stage Docker builds cho Go — minimal images, build caching.

workflow_skills.when: Trước khi design API mới hoặc refactor service
Khi implement feature mới hoặc breaking change
Khi có architectural decision quan trọng về service design
Khi implement theo task list
Khi deploy service lên production
Khi release version mới của service
Khi cần gradual rollout cho production traffic
Sau mỗi production deployment — monitor error rates và latency

stacks.name: ML Research with PyTorch
stacks.description: Bo kit cho nghien cuu ML va deep learning voi PyTorch. Bao gom experiment tracking, testing models, debugging training loops, va containerization.

core_skills.why: PyTorch best practices — model architecture, training loops, GPU management, CUDA.
Python patterns cho ML research — dataclasses, type hints, hydra configs.
pytest cho ML — test data pipelines, model output shapes, training step.
Literature review, paper reading, hypothesis forming cho research projects.
Debug training issues — loss divergence, gradient explosion, overfitting — cần hệ thống.
Reproducible training environment — CUDA version, dependencies pinned.

workflow_skills.when: Trước khi bắt đầu experiment mới hoặc architecture search
Khi lên plan cho experiment series hoặc research sprint
Khi chạy theo experiment plan
Trước khi submit paper hoặc release model checkpoint
Khi join research project mới hoặc tiếp tục work của người khác

stacks.name: ROS2 Autonomous Robot
stacks.description: Bo kit day du cho du an robot tu hanh voi ROS2. Bao gom debug, testing, planning, va docker patterns cho robotic software.

core_skills.why: ROS2 bugs thường phức tạp — node crash, TF issues, timing race conditions. Cần debug có hệ thống.
ROS2 codebase lớn với nhiều node. Cần tool để map dependencies và trace message flow.
Architecture robot system cần cân nhắc kỹ — sensor fusion, algorithm selection, safety constraints.
Plan rõ ràng trước khi implement node mới tránh integration hell.
ROS2 có gtest/pytest integration. Unit test cho algorithms trước khi test trên hardware.
Docker/dev container đảm bảo reproducible build environment cho ROS2 workspace.

workflow_skills.when: Khi bắt đầu implement node hoặc algorithm mới
Trước khi test trên robot thật — lỗi hardware nguy hiểm
Khi onboard vào codebase ROS2 mới hoặc debug cross-node issues
Khi hoàn thành thay đổi quan trọng đến safety/control logic
Sau khi feature ổn định và tested

stacks.name: Web Fullstack TypeScript
stacks.description: Bo kit cho web app fullstack voi TypeScript. Bao gom frontend patterns, API design, database, testing, security va deployment.

core_skills.why: React/Vue/Next.js patterns — component architecture, state management, performance.
REST/GraphQL API design — endpoints, versioning, error handling, validation.
PostgreSQL best practices — indexing, queries, connection pooling cho production.
Jest/Vitest TDD patterns cho TypeScript — unit, integration, mocking strategies.
Playwright/Cypress E2E tests cho critical user flows.
Web apps là target chính của attacks — OWASP, dependency CVEs, secrets.
Schema migrations an toàn — không break production, rollback plan.

workflow_skills.when: Trước khi design feature mới hoặc architecture change
Khi feature cần nhiều component thay đổi
Khi implement theo task list
Khi setup local dev environment hoặc production deployment
Khi deploy lên staging/production
Khi join project mới hoặc onboard team member
Sau khi hoàn thành implementation
